model_binding_compatibility.finding: The WorkerTemplate reference catalog returns every ModelBinding and exposes an OpenAI binding for Gemini CLI. The backend compiler correctly rejects this later, but the form cannot filter it before planning.

architecture_gate.required_boundary: Add a server-owned compatible WorkerTemplate ModelBinding query. It must resolve each ModelBinding to its model resource and return only bindings that satisfy the selected Worker Definition model requirement.
architecture_gate.web_contract: The model-reference field consumes returned ModelBinding references. It must not infer provider compatibility from a static Worker slug map or browser-visible resource names.
verification: Backend resolver test rejects an incompatible binding and returns only the compatible binding set.
Connect and Rust/TypeScript wire contracts preserve worker type and ModelBinding reference fields.
Browser test proves qa-primary-model is absent for Gemini CLI and a compatible Gemini binding is selectable when one exists.

browser_plan_recheck.visible_plan_issue: /spec: Worker template contains an invalid field.

conclusion: Gemini CLI has current local image, Runner, API, and browser runtime-selection evidence. It is not formally supported: no Pod, credential injection, terminal data-plane, or provider execution was authorized, and the WorkerTemplate model-binding compatibility filter is missing.
